etymology.description: 第(第) (dì, "prefix for ordinal numbers") is a prefix used to indicate the order or rank in a sequence, such as 'first', 'second', etc. 一(一) (yī, "one") specifies the number 'one'. 次(次) (cì, "time; occurrence") refers to an instance or occurrence of an event. Combined, 第(第) (dì, "prefix for ordinal numbers") + 一(一) (yī, "one") + 次(次) (cì, "time; occurrence") literally translates to the 'first instance' or 'first occurrence', thus meaning 'first time'.
etymology.interpretation: 第(第) (dì, "prefix for ordinal numbers") provides the ordinal sense, indicating position or sequence in a list. Following it, 一(一) (yī, "one") specifies the position as 'first', establishing the sequence. 次(次) (cì, "time; occurrence") then gives the notion of an event or instance. This leads to the understanding of 第一次 as 'the first event in a sequence' or simply 'the first time'.

examples.chinese: 这是我第一次去北京(這是我第一次去北京)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè shì wǒ dì yī cì qù Běijīng.
examples.english: This is my first time going to Beijing.

examples.chinese: 他第一次看到大海(他第一次看到大海)，感到非常兴奋(感到非常興奮)。
examples.pinyin: Tā dì yī cì kàn dào dàhǎi, gǎndào fēicháng xīngfèn.
examples.english: He felt very excited when he saw the sea for the first time.

examples.chinese: 我们第一次见面(我們第一次見面)，是在一个朋友的聚会(是在一個朋友的聚會)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen dì yī cì jiànmiàn, shì zài yī gè péngyǒu de jùhuì.
examples.english: The first time we met was at a friend's party.
